hypothesis: If fragment-based and combined similarity/string constraints, especially when implemented adaptively, are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet-fission candidate generation, then the generated molecules will exhibit significantly higher diversity across representation-, SMILES n-gram-, fragment-, and scaffold-based metrics and reduced overrepresentation of patterns compared to baseline unconstrained runs while retaining comparable singlet-fission ΔE_SF optimization performance.
